About me

Rebecca Huang is an animation director and story artist based in New York City. She received her Master of Fine Arts degree in Computer Arts from the School of Visual Arts (SVA). Before she came to her senses and started pursuing a creative career full-time, she worked in sales and marketing strategies at Google for three years. While this experience trained her business acumen, it also made her realize how much she longed to draw, create and tell stories. She worked with Baboon Animation in New York on preproduction for the preschool series Bek and the Bunnies. In 2020, she collaborated with Japanese studio Griot Groove to write the first season of a 12-episode anime series, The Deep Hunter. In 2023, she led a team of 20 animators and artists to direct an animated short film “the Human Fossil.”

Rebecca has studied and worked in Singapore, Tokyo, Shanghai and New York. Her hair has been red since 2021.
